methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Duranium is a pre-seed climate and energy company that has raised $2M+. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers, with notable private-stage companies including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the peer-median method is not applicable here due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the sector—only 19 same-sector peers exist overall, but none yield three or more datapoints when filtered to stage and valuation tier.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using the $2M total raised as a valuation proxy for the pre-seed stage, a standard 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next funding round implies a valuation of $5M. This multiplier reflects typical pre-seed to seed-stage progression observed across frontier-tech financing patterns.

Given the single-method triangulation, the analysis supports a valuation range of $4M to $6M, with a median of $5M. The primary uncertainty stems from the absence of tight peer comparables at this stage in the climate and energy space, which limits confidence in adjustments for company-specific risk factors or differentiation. The range brackets the stage-progression output to account for this constraint.
